cmd.refresh.title: Claude Usage: Refresh now
cmd.openState.title: Claude Usage: Open state file
cmd.openPanel.title: Claude Usage: Open panel (SVG ring)
cmd.cycleStyle.title: Claude Usage: Cycle status bar style
cmd.setStyle.title: Claude Usage: Set status bar style
cmd.toggleAlert.title: Claude Usage: Toggle burn rate alert
config.title: Claude Code Usage Bar
config.stateFilePath.desc: Path to the state file written by the Claude Code statusline. Empty = `~/.claude/usage-state.json`.
config.accountType.desc: Account type. On **subscription**, the dollar cost is only the API-price equivalent (a reference for how much you save) — it is not a charge, so there is no cost cap or alert. On **api**, the cost is real.
config.accountType.enum.auto: Detect automatically (subscription if plan limits are present)
config.accountType.enum.subscription: Subscription (Pro/Max): you already pay the monthly fee — the $ is just an informational 'API equivalent', with no cost cap or alert. The focus is on plan time/limits.
config.accountType.enum.api: API/pay-as-you-go: the $ is real money, with cost cap and alert.
config.mode.desc: How to display. **auto** (recommended) chooses based on what Claude Code returns: subscription accounts have plan limits (`rate_limits`) and show 5h/7d; API/pay-as-you-go accounts have no limit and show cost `$` with the ring reflecting context.
config.mode.enum.auto: Detect automatically: subscriber (Pro/Max) shows 5h/7d limits; API account shows $ cost
config.mode.enum.subscriber: Force subscriber mode: ring = 5h window, number = 7d window (same as /usage)
config.mode.enum.cost: Force cost mode: ring = context, number = accumulated session $ cost
config.costCapUsd.desc: Cost cap (USD) per session, used **only in cost mode / API accounts** to color the indicator as spending approaches this value. `0` disables the cost warning.
config.barStyle.desc: Indicator style **in the status bar**. For the large SVG ring, open the panel (click the item or run the _Claude Usage: Open panel_ command).
config.warnThreshold.desc: Percentage above which the indicator turns yellow.
config.errorThreshold.desc: Percentage above which the indicator turns red.
config.alignment.desc: Side of the status bar where the indicator appears.
config.priority.desc: Item priority in the status bar (higher = closer to the edge).
config.staleAfterSeconds.desc: Window in which the statusline data is considered fresh. Beyond that, ccusage is used.
config.ccusageCommand.desc: Command to fetch the session block (5h). Empty = `npx -y ccusage@latest blocks --active --json`. Point to a globally installed `ccusage` to avoid npx latency (e.g. `ccusage blocks --active --json`).
config.ccusageRefreshSeconds.desc: How often (seconds) to refresh the ccusage data.
config.useOAuthUsage.desc: Use the official `api/oauth/usage` endpoint (the same source as `/usage`) as the primary source — it shows the **real quota** for the 5h session and the week, identical to `/usage`. It reads the OAuth token from the macOS Keychain (service `Claude Code-credentials`); the token stays local and is only sent to `api.anthropic.com`. When available, the ring becomes the real quota.
config.oauthRefreshSeconds.desc: How often (seconds) to query the oauth/usage endpoint.
config.burnRateAlertEnabled.desc: Warn when the spending pace is projected to exceed a limit before reset (cost, $/h, or plan limits).
config.burnRateMaxPerHour.desc: Alert when the pace (`$/h`) goes above this value. `0` disables this specific trigger.
config.alertCooldownMinutes.desc: Minimum time (minutes) between alert notifications, to avoid repeating too often.
config.colorByProjection.desc: Color the indicator by the **projected** overflow (not just the current value). The color uses the worse of current usage and the projection up to reset.
config.intenseTokensPerMin.desc: Tokens/min pace considered 'intense' (= 100% on the projection color) for **subscription accounts in the app**, where there is no real plan limit. Used only as an intensity proxy.
config.sessionTokenCap.desc: Cap for **tokens per 5h session** (e.g. `150000000` for 150M). When set, the indicator projects — at the current pace — how many tokens you will have used by the end of the window and alerts/colors if the projection exceeds the cap, with an ETA ('exceeds in ~Xmin'). `0` disables it. Useful on subscriptions, where there is no official token limit.
config.resetWarningMinutes.desc: Warn when this much time (minutes) remains before the **5h session resets** — useful for lining up heavy work right after the new window. `0` disables the warning.
config.lowQuotaThreshold.desc: Warn (once per window) when **less than this % of quota remains** in the 5h session or the 7d week — only with real data (oauth/statusline). Re-arms by itself when the quota recovers. `0` disables the warning.
config.ringTheme.desc: Color theme for the **ring and bars** in the panel. **semaforo** (default) keeps the current behavior (green/yellow/red depending on level). **claude** uses the Claude orange (`#D97757`) as the base color. **mono**/**custom** use the color defined in `#claudeUsageBar.ringColor#`. In all themes, the **critical** level (overflow) stays **red** so the alert signal is not lost.
config.ringTheme.enum.semaforo: Traffic light (default): color by level — green up to the warning, yellow, red at critical.
config.ringTheme.enum.claude: Claude: Claude orange (#D97757) as a single color; still turns red at the critical level.
config.ringTheme.enum.mono: Mono: a single color defined in ringColor; still turns red at the critical level.
config.ringTheme.enum.custom: Custom: same as mono (alias) — uses the ringColor color; still turns red at the critical level.
config.ringColor.desc: Color (hex `#RRGGBB`) of the ring/bars when `#claudeUsageBar.ringTheme#` is **mono** or **custom**. Ignored in other themes. If the value is not a valid 6-digit hex, it is discarded (falls back to the default) so the drawing is not broken.
config.blockSummaryEnabled.desc: When a **5h session closes** (when the window resets), show a summary of what it consumed (quota, tokens, and peak equivalent cost).
config.statusCheckEnabled.desc: Monitor Anthropic status (`status.claude.com`) and show the **Status** tab in the panel.
config.statusBadgeEnabled.desc: Show a badge (☁) on the status bar indicator when there is an incident/degradation at Anthropic.
config.statusNotifyEnabled.desc: Notify (once per incident) when a new problem appears in the Anthropic ecosystem.
extension.description: [Unofficial] Claude Code session usage (5h/7d limits, tokens, and cost) in the status bar — a progress ring like /usage — plus the status of the Anthropic ecosystem (status.claude.com).
config.exportStateEnabled.desc: Write a local JSON file with current usage (remaining 5h/7d quota, source and trustworthiness) for **agents/scripts** to read — e.g. to stop auto-mode when remaining quota gets low. Atomic write, no token, no external upload.
config.exportStatePath.desc: Path of the export file. Empty = `~/.claude/usage-bar.json` (resolves on Windows/macOS/Linux). Accepts `~` for home.
